notes: Behavior-first regression suite for Dex maintainer routing, schema hygiene, release gates, and skill-eval orchestration. Cases must prove agent behavior with artifacts or explicit evidence, not just mention workflow terms.

evals.prompt: Validate the Codex dex skill folder with the system skill-creator quick validator.
evals.expected_output: The Codex skill frontmatter uses only supported metadata and the usage syntax lives in the body.
assertions: System quick_validate.py passes for .agents/skills/dex.
SKILL.md keeps release and eval syntax visible outside frontmatter.
No argument-hint frontmatter key remains in the Codex skill.

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to eval .agents/skills/dex. Before running any clean-context evaluator, check whether the eval suite itself is healthy and gradeable.
evals.expected_output: The agent validates the eval suite first, records its health, fixes blocking suite issues before forward runs, and only then launches clean-context evaluation.
assertions: Runs deterministic suite-health checks before any codex exec or subagent forward run.
Checks JSON parseability, required coverage, required evidence, duplicate IDs, and .agents/.claude mirroring.
Blocks or repairs the run if the eval suite is missing required coverage or contains ungradeable assertions.
Records the suite-health result in the round artifact or final eval summary.
required_evidence: decision to proceed, block, or repair
artifact or summary entry recorded before forward-run output

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to eval plugins/design/skills/crux. Follow the OpenAI eval-skills pattern: prompt rows, codex exec JSONL traces, deterministic checks, and schema-constrained rubric grading.
evals.expected_output: The agent records each runnable case as prompt -> JSONL trace/artifacts -> deterministic checks -> optional rubric JSON -> score, and stores the run record under .dex/evals/.
assertions: Uses `codex exec --json` for forward-test runs and saves stdout as a .jsonl trace.
Grades objective behavior from JSONL events or artifacts before model-assisted judgment.
Parses command behavior from `command_execution` events and token/cost data from `turn.completed` events when present.
Uses `--output-schema` for qualitative rubric judging and saves the rubric JSON.
Records score and overall_pass in the case run record.
required_evidence: rubric JSON path or explicit deterministic-only skip

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to eval plugins/design/skills/crux rounds=2 baseline=previous.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads eval.md, loads skill-creator before repair, snapshots the target skill, designs or refreshes the relevant eval suite, runs clean-context evals, grades, diagnoses, repairs, and reruns.
assertions: Routes to ./eval.md rather than ./release.md.
Mentions skill-creator as the repair method.
Designs or refreshes relevant eval cases before touching the target skill.
Includes clean-context evals through fresh subagents or codex exec.
Records artifacts under .dex/evals/<skill-name>/<timestamp>/round-N/.

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to evaluate a new local skill at plugins/tools/skills/example-new-skill. It has no evals yet. Do not repair the skill until the eval suite exists.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats the missing eval suite as setup work, creates or proposes concrete eval cases first, then stops or continues to evaluation only after the suite is inspectable.
assertions: Classifies the target as new or baseline=none unless a snapshot is requested.
Creates or proposes eval coverage before repair.
Includes explicit trigger, implicit trigger, contextual trigger, negative-control, artifact, and repair-regression placeholders.
Does not edit the target skill before the eval suite exists.
required_evidence: target skill snapshot or missing-target note
new eval suite path or full proposed eval JSON
no target-skill edit before eval design

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to eval plugins/dev/skills/blade because the last Blade change may have weakened audit quality. Repair anything that fails.
evals.expected_output: The agent first snapshots the target, then inspects or writes the eval suite that will judge Blade audit quality before editing Blade skill files.
assertions: Names eval-suite design as its own phase before Evaluate or Repair.
Inspects existing evals, fixtures, scripts, and known failures before running or repairing.
Adds or updates concrete cases for missing Blade audit coverage before touching the target skill.
Does not discover the eval criteria only after making the repair.

must_include_any: design or refresh the relevant eval suite
must_not_include_any: run a quick eval first, then decide what to test

required_evidence: new or refreshed Blade audit case
target files unchanged until eval design completes

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to eval plugins/design/skills/diverge. The target skill has uncommitted local edits. Benchmark the repair without losing the dirty work.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses baseline=snapshot, preserves local edits, stores the pre-repair snapshot, and compares repaired behavior against that snapshot rather than previous committed history.
assertions: Detects dirty target state.
Uses baseline=snapshot unless the user overrides it.
Stores a pre-repair snapshot under .dex/evals/diverge/<timestamp>/round-1/.
Does not revert or discard user edits.
Records benchmark delta against the snapshot.
required_evidence: git status or diff evidence for target
statement that dirty work was preserved

evals.prompt: The Blade skill feels weaker after the last change. Benchmark it against the prior version and repair whatever fails.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats benchmark and repair language as dex eval intent, not as a release.
assertions: Routes benchmark/repair intent to ./eval.md.
Uses baseline=previous for an existing clean skill unless overridden.
Runs or proposes a previous-vs-current comparison.
Does not propose a plugin release before eval evidence exists.
required_evidence: release recommendation blocked or deferred until eval passes

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to eval plugins/design/skills/crux. Its existing eval has an assertion that always passes and another that requires a hidden answer. Fix the eval process before changing the skill.
evals.expected_output: The agent diagnoses the suite as a bad eval, rewrites the bad assertions into observable checks, reruns or restates the corrected suite, and only then considers skill repair.
assertions: Labels the primary failure as missing fixture or bad eval.
Fixes over-broad or hidden-answer assertions first.
Does not blame the target skill before the eval is gradeable.
Adds a regression case for the bad-eval failure.
required_evidence: no target skill edit before eval fix

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to release dev minor.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads release.md and starts with README/release-doc and preflight gates before any version bump.
assertions: Routes to ./release.md rather than ./eval.md.
Checks main branch and clean worktree before release mutation.
Runs README/release-doc parity gate before tagging.
required_evidence: no version bump before gates pass

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill: release this. The branch changes plugins/design/skills/crux and plugins/dev/skills/blade.
evals.expected_output: The agent inspects the branch diff, maps changes to both design and dev plugins, and plans separate per-plugin release commits/tags instead of one combined release.
assertions: Does not infer a single release target from the phrase release this.
Inspects diff before selecting plugin targets.
Names both design and dev as affected release surfaces.
Plans separate release commits and tags per plugin.
required_evidence: separate release plan for design and dev

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to release tools patch, but the repo has an unrelated untracked file and unstaged README changes.
evals.expected_output: The agent stops before mutating version metadata, reports the dirty worktree, and asks for cleanup or explicit scope rather than staging unrelated files.
assertions: Runs worktree cleanliness preflight before version bumps.
Names the dirty files.
Does not stage unrelated files.
Does not edit manifests or create tags.

evals.prompt: Use the dex skill to release core minor after the plugin manifests move from 1.0.2 to 1.1.0. README.md has a current-version table.
evals.expected_output: The agent updates the selected plugin's Claude and Codex manifests, the Claude marketplace metadata, and README.md's current-version table in the release commit before tagging.
assertions: Treats README current-version parity as part of the release mutation, not a separate optional follow-up.
Updates the README row for the released plugin to the new version.
Asserts the selected plugin README row before tagging.
Stages README.md with the manifest and marketplace version files.
Does not tag or publish while README.md still shows the previous plugin version.
required_evidence: selected README row assertion before tag
no tag before README parity passes

evals.expected_output: The agent asks whether to run a plugin release or a skill eval.
assertions: Does not assume release.
Does not assume eval.
Asks for the missing mode.
required_evidence: single clarifying question naming release and eval

evals.prompt: Rewrite the README paragraph for the design plugin so it is shorter and clearer.
evals.expected_output: The dex eval workflow should not be invoked unless the user asks for release, skill eval, benchmarking, or repair.
assertions: Does not route to ./eval.md.
Does not create .dex/evals artifacts.
Uses ordinary writing/editing workflow unless release intent appears.
required_evidence: direct README edit or proposed copy

evals.prompt: Tighten the first paragraph of plugins/design/skills/present/SKILL.md. Do not run a full benchmark.
evals.expected_output: The agent performs a narrow docs edit without invoking /dex eval because the user explicitly ruled out a benchmark.
assertions: Does not route to ./eval.md.
Does not create .dex/evals artifacts.
Keeps the edit scoped to the requested paragraph.
May run quick validation if the edited skill needs metadata checking.
required_evidence: no benchmark language in final answer
